items.title: RT menaka doshi: India’s AI Sovereignty Challenge: Winning Without Owning the Whole Stack The world’s fifth-largest economy has few strengths in the...
items.description_html:
RT menaka doshi
India’s AI Sovereignty Challenge: Winning Without Owning the Whole Stack

The world’s fifth-largest economy has few strengths in the AI ecosystem. It is broadly self-sufficient in energy but imports all advanced AI chips, accounts for only about 3% of global data-center capacity and has yet to produce a globally competitive frontier model.

At first glance, the conclusion appears obvious: India is behind.

That inference deserves more nuance, Srikanth Velamakanni (@srikanth) and Ganesh Ramakrishnan (@ganramkr) argued when they joined me on Bloomberg’s new Emerging podcast.

Watch here 👇
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/videos/2026-07-09/can-india-catch-up-in-the-ai-race-video?cmpid=india-edition&utm_campaign=india-edition&utm_medium=email&utm_source=newsletter&utm_term=260709&utm_content=6348

items.title: RT menaka doshi: Why does a country that gave the world the concept of zero now invest so little in creating new knowledge? There is no shortage of ex...
items.description_html:
RT menaka doshi
Why does a country that gave the world the concept of zero now invest so little in creating new knowledge? There is no shortage of explanations.

Chief Economic Adviser V. Anantha Nageswaran has pointed to colonial deindustrialization, decades of protectionism, premature financialization and policy uncertainty. Industrialist Naushad Forbes argues the deeper problem is insufficient competition, which has reduced the pressure on companies to innovate.

Ajai Chowdhry, co-founder of HCL Infosystems, tells me it’s because India has failed to become a product nation.

“When you design and make products in India, that’s when you will see R&D happen. In service businesses, people don’t invest in deep research,” said Chowdhry, who now serves as chairman of the National Quantum Mission.

Lessons From India’s Big Antibiotic Breakthrough 👇

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/newsletters/2026-07-02/indian-innovation-shows-proof-of-life-with-wockhardt-s-antibiotic-breakthrough?srnd=undefined

items.title: "What is the point of India spending billions on its image, like raising swanky airports? Is the idea that someone would land at such an airport, be b...
items.description_html:
"What is the point of India spending billions on its image, like raising swanky airports?

Is the idea that someone would land at such an airport, be blown away by it, and then when he is stuck in the hell hole of the Indian city, inhaling poisonous air, he is going to think how fine the airport was?

What is the point of brand-building exercises when anyone who lands here can see within five minutes that things are a mess, we do not know the meaning of planning, and we are a danger to ourselves?"

Good one by @manujosephsan
https://manujoseph.substack.com/p/what-rising-wrong-side-driving-says?triedRedirect=true

items.title: RT Persistence Capital: We are pleasantly surprised by Q1FY27 business updates so far: Credit growth - accelerating and healthy Consumer (Retailers, a...
items.description_html:
RT Persistence Capital
We are pleasantly surprised by Q1FY27 business updates so far:

Credit growth - accelerating and healthy

Consumer (Retailers, auto OEMs, jewelers, FMCG, real estate) - YoY growth robust despite the overhang of war in Q1

USD$ services exports - YoY growth remains healthy despite deflationary impact of GenAI. Leasing data, office occupancy, square foot addition suggest that India is moving from back office to middle office. IT services revenue growth remains lackluster but GCCs (so far) are more than picking up the slack

Sunrise industries galore - Precision manufacturing, drones, specialty chemicals, CDMOs etc

Capital goods - Domestic business accelerating as India builds domestic DC footprint

Markets haven't moved for 22 months now in local currency terms. Valuations have time corrected. Growth trajectory remains healthy and more importantly, untethered to the AI capex build-out.

Hard to argue that India isn't interesting again for FIIs

items.title: Learning of the day before going to sleep What are Solid State Transformers? 1. Solid-state transformers (SSTs) are advanced power devices that replac...
items.description_html:
Learning of the day before going to sleep

What are Solid State Transformers?

1. Solid-state transformers (SSTs) are advanced power devices that replace traditional, heavy copper-and-iron transformers with electronic circuitry. They use semiconductor switches and high-frequency internal components to change voltage.

2. Solid State Transformers are devices which enable real-time monitoring and control — something legacy transformers cannot do because it’s been all steel and copper.

3. Traditional transformers are passive, bulky blocks of metal that only adjust voltage. By contrast, SSTs act as the "smart router" of the electrical grid.

4.Incredible Shrinkage: SSTs operate at much higher frequencies than the normal grid (which operates at 50 or 60 Hz). The higher the frequency, the smaller the transformer core can be. This shrinks their size and weight by up to 50%, making them ideal for dense urban areas and modern data centers.

5. Two-Way Power Flow: Traditional transformers only allow power to flow in one direction. SSTs allow bidirectional power flow, meaning power can travel both to and from devices. This makes them essential for electric vehicles that need to feed power back to the grid and for homes with solar panels.

6. Smart Grid Control: SSTs use software and microchips to actively manage electricity. If the voltage drops, they adjust it instantly. They can filter out "dirty" power (like electrical noise or surges) to protect sensitive electronics, acting like a giant surge protector and regulator in one.

Must read article on Transformer shortage here

https://ig.ft.com/transformers/

items.title: The 10 Lakh crore Thermal Power Capex boom https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Funuk-of7t8&t=3018s How thermal Power is generated (1).Coal is used as fuel...
items.description_html:
The 10 Lakh crore Thermal Power Capex boom

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Funuk-of7t8&t=3018s

How thermal Power is generated

(1).Coal is used as fuel which is burned in a boiler to create intense heat

(2). That heat boils water into high-pressure, high-temperature steam

(3). The steam then rushes through and spins a turbine

(4). Converting the heat energy into spinning mechanical motion. This spinning turbine drives a generator.

(5) Which actually produces the electricity. The voltage is then stepped up by a transformer

(6) So the power can travel long distances, and finally it reaches the power grid.

(7) Grid delivers electricity to homes and industries.

items.description_html:
India wants to keep expanding oil refining capacities to hedge upon the global chokepoints

The current capex pipeline that is there -

Barmer is the one that got commissioned. The order book behind the 258 → 310 MMTPA path is what actually funds the ecosystem:

1) Panipat P-25 (IOCL): 15 → 25 MMTPA
2) Cauvery Basin Refinery (CPCL): new 9 MMTPA grassroot
3) Visakh Refinery Modernisation (VRMP) (HPCL)
4) Barauni (IOCL): new ~180,000 bpd crude unit (older units to be decommissioned)
5) Numaligarh (NRL, OIL group): +120,000 bpd expansion
6) Gujarat refinery (IOCL): +86,000 bpd
7) Bina & Mumbai (BPCL): unit additions + Bina petchem
8) Paradip petrochemical complex (IOCL)
9) Andhra Refinery (BPCL): new grassroot
10) Ratnagiri West Coast (RRPCL — IOCL/BPCL/HPCL, ± Aramco/ADNOC): the 60 MMTPA long-shot mega-project still on the map

Also we have attached here the detailed value chain that is there which benefits with this capex cycle.

items.title: Tam for EMS industry India is emerging as a key global electronics manufacturing hub, with the EMS industry expected to grow at 25-30% CAGR over FY24-...
items.description_html:
Tam for EMS industry

India is emerging as a key global electronics manufacturing hub, with the EMS industry expected to grow at 25-30% CAGR over FY24-29E.

Growth is driven by the China+1 shift, electronics exports reaching ~USD48b in FY26 (+25% YoY), and policy support through PLI, INR400b ECMS and semiconductor initiatives.

With India accounting for only 4-5% of the global EMS market, increasing component localization and export-led manufacturing provide a long runway for growth.

EMS companies which will go backwards into component manufacturing are the ones where complete integrated business models will be created

items.title: Agentic routing is such an interesting study. Built a basic agent that does planning on Opus 4 but reasons on 4.6 through a tool call (skill call). Du...
items.description_html: Agentic routing is such an interesting study. Built a basic agent that does planning on Opus 4 but reasons on 4.6 through a tool call (skill call). Due to lack of document grounding, I embedded corpus into system prompt. Worked well. Second stage, enabled managed RAG for grounding & agent stated skipping tool call entirely, no skill calls. It treated grounded data as a higher evidence deterministic choice & skipped skill call. Tweaked tool description, still no skill call. Changed skill description matching to tool call & bingo!! Agent skipped grounding, entered planning mode & made a tool call. Evals for Agentic behavior is literally out paces everything in this field. What's your such key learning from a recent build?

items.title: A lot of defense to IT SIs right now is based on assumption that enterprise clients will not open their systems, data & processes to external AI solut...
items.description_html: A lot of defense to IT SIs right now is based on assumption that enterprise clients will not open their systems, data & processes to external AI solutions (Claude, OpenAi). However, this defense collapses the moment we have a precedence from a big enterprise opening up just the way enterprises opened up to cloud after a decade long friction. The moment this starts happening, the JVs OpenAi & Claude have formed will make a killing. And, what will take this wall down? For e.g., enterprises are currently very upset after SAP API policy that prohibited clients from using their own data & sending it to external tools via APIs to architect a solution outside of SAP. This can be the 1st hammer in the wall. One wrong step & clients will find ways to disconnect with enterprise OEMs & will be willing to trust(with an agreement perhaps) likes of Claude & OpenAi. The moment this happens, it is done!! This is the biggest risk I foresee.

items.title: Vikas bhai, I agree that change matters more than history. In fact, Laurus is a great example of why investors shouldn't anchor on the past. The disti...
items.description_html:
Vikas bhai, I agree that change matters more than history. In fact, Laurus is a great example of why investors shouldn't anchor on the past.

The distinction I would make is between "capability" and "validated capability".

Laurus in 2019 had already built impressive scientific, manufacturing and execution capabilities. What the market questioned was whether those capabilities would ever translate into superior economics. That was the leap of faith, which is why the valuation remained modest. When I first bought it, I paid just about 1.5x P/S. That was Dec '19.

What changed over the next few years wasn't just the narrative. The business established an economic reference point. Once ROCE moved to 40% in FY21 and remained well above historical levels in FY22 and FY23, investors had tangible evidence that the operating system could convert years of investment into exceptional returns on capital. Those returns were achieved after fully expensing R&D through the P&L, with no capitalisation to flatter the numbers. From that point onwards, investors were no longer underwriting possibility. They were underwriting replication.

Biocon is different. R&D is not 100% expensed out. I have no doubt about the science, manufacturing capability or strategic ambition. My difficulty is that I don't yet have an equivalent economic reference point. The business has not yet demonstrated a sustained period of returns that allows me to confidently visualise what mature economics look like after fully expensing its investments in capability. Without that evidence, I find it difficult to distinguish between "latent capability" and "demonstrated capability".

That's also why I don't think Syngene provides the same template as leading CRDMOs such as Anthem (peak ROCE ~50%; currently ~30%). It has built outstanding capabilities over many years, but it hasn't consistently translated those capabilities into the kind of economics that establish a reliable benchmark for future compounding.

For me, investing is less about believing that a business can become exceptional than about identifying the point at which the system has shown that it has become exceptional. Laurus crossed that line between FY21 and FY23. The valuation at the next low point was double my initial entry in 2019, but post-validation, that 3x P/S was good enough for me to load it again. That 1.5x low of 2019 became 3x sales at the 2023 low cycle point. We don't know what the next low would mean in terms of P/S or in which year that next low cycle will happen, but it will probably be much higher than the previous low of 3x sales seen in 2023.

The question I keep asking on Biocon is therefore not whether management can execute. It is much simpler.

What is the strongest economic reference point this system has ever demonstrated, and what evidence suggests those economics are repeatable rather than aspirational?

Until I can answer that with confidence, I think the odds of a Laurus-style rerating are lower. I hope your conviction beats my scepticism.

We all respect @kiranshaw for her contrarian thinking and entrepreneurial courage. Those qualities built a globally respected enterprise. The remaining challenge is converting that capability into the kind of sustained economics that create enduring shareholder wealth. That's ultimately what markets reward.


Vikas Khemani: @unseenvalue @sudheerindia1 Sajal bhai, agreed. Their past is not great on capital allocation and RoCE. But notice “change”. @kiranshaw has committed to no capex till utilisation picks up on last few calls. Driving efficiency on all fronts. No one believed Laurus and their ability to generate return 3 years

items.title: If a normal 20% annual move already generates gains comparable to your invested capital, that’s better value than making a new decision (selling X to...
items.description_html:
If a normal 20% annual move already generates gains comparable to your invested capital, that’s better value than making a new decision (selling X to switch into Y). A 40% move is roughly equivalent to doubling your “average up” price. You also avoid the risks of a new decision, along with taxes and transaction costs.
It’s not easy to find great management, strong economics in terms of incremental ROCE, sustainable runway of reinvestment, industry tailwinds, satisfied customers and employee … all at a low P/S (at entry or last average up). That’s why Munger said, ‘Big money is in the waiting, not the buying and selling.’
That’s how I think about my Laurus allocation today after a 23× return over 6.5 years and a 4× from the 2023 doubling-down levels. A “boring” 20% annual move would be about 5× the 2019 allocation and 1× the 2023 allocation. I’d be very content with that.
To each their own. Know your contentment level and don’t play the Investing game using someone else’s lens. Play your own game!

items.title: RT Sagar Awatade: I don't know who else to tell this to, so I am going to tell my story here. Every day is a struggle for a young business, but the la...
items.description_html:
RT Sagar Awatade
I don't know who else to tell this to, so I am going to tell my story here.

Every day is a struggle for a young business, but the last few months have been harder than usual.

We are a small Indian company. For more than ten years we have been building a homegrown brand in a product category dominated by big foreign players.

There are almost no Indian names in this space. We set out to be one.

We started in 2014. Over the years we began making parts in India instead of just importing, and we started selling in the US, Dubai, Nepal, Malaysia and South Africa.

We showed up at global trade fairs to represent an Indian brand on the world stage.

In 2023 we changed the import code we use for our product. We did not do this quietly. Every shipment was declared. Nothing was hidden. We didn't invent our approach.

We followed written professional advice and the way this product is treated in markets around the world.

And now we are facing a government demand running into tens of crores in duty recovery and penalties, plus personal penalties on the founders and even on an employee.

For a company our size, this is not a fine we can pay and move on from. This ends us.

We have not run from any of this. I am not built like that. It is not how I was raised. We have written to the authorities, met officials in person, and we have now filed a writ in the High Court.

All we are asking for is a fair treatment.

I set out to build in India and sell to the world. I am asking only that the system back honest founders trying to compete globally, instead of breaking them.

The process is the process, and it exists for a reason. But process should not feel like punishment.

From where I am standing today, it does.
I am not giving up. I have worked too hard for this. If you have read this far, please share it. If you know someone who can help, point them my way. Help me get the word out.

2. CAD used in PCB- Spell from @PcbCupid is one step in this direction with a larger vision of building our own CAD tools for PCB. Team has made good progress on autorouting recently.

3. Test and Measurement Tools - Huge gap in this space and almostly entirely imported products today. TDR used in impedance measurment for example is a low hanging fruit in PCB space if someone wants to start.

All of these are important for building a strong electronics ecosystem in 🇮🇳

items.title: Exactly 10 years ago in May 2016, i went to a session on bootstrapping at TiE event in Santa Clara. That changed my life. When i spent time in bay are...
items.description_html:
Exactly 10 years ago in May 2016, i went to a session on bootstrapping at TiE event in Santa Clara. That changed my life.

When i spent time in bay area working on advanced sensing technologies in semicon space, everything was there in life(money, granted patents, travelling to new country every month, wife, so called good life) but it still felt empty within.

Was burning a lot of midnight oil trying to build something, failed twice trying to build in US. Those failures hit hard and made me realise that everthing that i had was only beacuse of the company that i was working for, i was zero outside of it.

During this phase is when i first met @svembu ji, did not know who he was, did not know about zoho. He was wearing a plain simple green kurtha, jeans and slippers gving a session on bootstraping his company. I asked "can we bootstrap a hardware business too ?" And he said " why not ? "

This along with zoho story, made my conviction of moving back to India stronger, it took almost a week for me to digest what zoho was and what was built without raising a single dime from VCs and i felt so proud and blessed to have met the man behind.

Moved back to India same year in October. It was only about building something here which makes sense in long term, something which was ignored by people for a long time.

Ofcourse it came at a cost, divorce, hard time telling people not to worry about me or my personal life, making sure that my parents are not suffering, isolating all the peronal stuff with the vision of building strong electronics manufacutring ecosystem in India.

10 years FF- we are a strong team of 200+, bootstrapped, growing fast, building factories in India which can compete globally soon. On a personal level, i still hardly spend few thousand rupess everymonth, lead a minimal life, have a loving wife who belives in me and a kid who is growing fast, parents who are proud that i am doing something in India.

There is no multimillion dollars in my bank as safety, my kid and wife are Indians with Indian passport and my parents were teachers/avg govt employee without any background in business.

I dont feel empty inside me, anymore. Fire inside me is for building India's electronics manufacturing capacity that can compete globally, a strong ecosystem for next gen to build products from India and a strong team that can run this beyond me.

Jai Hind!🇮🇳

items.title: RT Anil Agarwal: It is painful to see India face the adverse consequences of a war we have nothing to do with, particularly because of raw material se...
items.description_html:
RT Anil Agarwal
It is painful to see India face the adverse consequences of a war we have nothing to do with, particularly because of raw material security. We don’t have to import 90% of our oil, or 95% of our copper or 99.5% of our gold or any other metal resources. Mother Earth has given us the best geology. Last 40 years of practice in the minerals, metals and oil industry have taught me this. Now is the time, with this positive government, to give entrepreneurs freedom.

At just 19, I came to Bombay from Bihar and built a company in this industry from scratch. Vedanta was able to acquire Hindustan Zinc and BALCO under a privatisation programme which was never completed. 26% and 49% stakes are still held by the government and not been transferred as per a predetermined agreement. Vedanta also acquired ONGC Oil and Gas asset via UK’s Cairn and Sesa Goa Iron ore from Japan’s Mitsui.

In each one, the ambition and goal was to increase the production so much that India wouldn’t need to import but, in fact export. In Hindustan Zinc and BALCO, we succeeded. We increased zinc production 10 times, 20 times in aluminium. In the process, more than 1,000 companies have come up for processing this raw material. And Vedanta has contributed Rs 4.5 lakh crore to the exchequer in the last ten years. In oil and gas, our vision is to produce 1 million barrels per day. In iron ore, my vision is to produce 100 million tonnes, 33% of today’s production level. There are many more underperforming government assets that can perform the same way.

Three or four decades ago, there was no know-how, no experts, no finance in this industry. It took a lot of determination. Vedanta raised 35 billion USD from abroad and invested in India. It brought experts and technology. Because of that, Hindustan Zinc can also produce silver and fertiliser, and we are working very hard to start producing critical minerals very soon, something we were told would never happen in India.

Globally, only few very large companies engage in mining as it is very difficult without scale. Vedanta should be for India what Rio Tinto and BHP are for Australia or Vale is for Brazil. Other entrepreneurs and companies must create new Vedantas.

Most of the advanced countries have become prosperous by developing natural resources, including oil and gas. In India, the system has to stop creating hurdles. It needs to take a long-term view, as this government is not revenue-minded but, production-minded. It must facilitate. Instead of notices and judicial activity, all businesses need to receive recognition and respect. Benefit of doubt should pass onto them.

In India, we have economic heroes who can raise production equal to the biggest and best in the world. We should not allow tunnel vision or outdated mindset to keep us small and dependent. Some may remember the case of Rio Tinto which had invested in a diamond mine in Madhya Pradesh. It could have made India a global hub for diamond production, but they exited because of a complex, interfering and discouraging system.

The most important thing is trust. Keep the system simple, keep regulation limited and you eliminate the root cause of wrongdoing. We should have self-certification instead of lengthy approvals and clearances. The Government issues the rulebook and entrepreneurs strictly comply, subject to audit. Constant enquiry and interference only discourage enterprise. Also, the mindset of discrimination between public sector and private sector should change.

This year my family had the biggest tragedy. It was heartening to hear the words of the Prime Minister who said, “You must stay strong and continue to do work which is important for India.” That is the motivation of my life. I have the blessings of my grandfather and grandmother, who spent 40 years in Vrindavan in a small hut, in Manav Seva Sangh with their guruji, Swami Sharnanand Ji Maharaj. They always advised me, if you have wealth, you must use it for society. If you don’t have wealth, you must always help society with your hand. We all are willing to work hard, take risks and ensure that India becomes self-sufficient. The system should encourage us to deliver for our society and nation; this will eradicate unemployment and encourage women empowerment. This sector has given the maximum revenue to the exchequer in India and in the world for nation-building. It will help in making our country a developed nation, as envisioned by our Prime Minister. Let’s make India & Indians proud.

items.title: Our entire country's 🇮🇳 production of bare pcb is equivalent of jlc capacity. If you take only multilayer, than its worse than this. Jlc is not ...
items.description_html:
Our entire country's 🇮🇳 production of bare pcb is equivalent of jlc capacity.
If you take only multilayer, than its worse than this.

Jlc is not even in the top 50 in 🇨🇳.

Let that sink in...

China was closed for 15days last month, we were overbooked by 3-4x and we couldn't get the machines for the bottleneck process on time.

Raw materials, capital goods everything has to come from 🇨🇳 and it takes time.

Factory in china can get these machines in 7 days to expand, we need to wait atleast 45-60 days.

What you see as an outcome is delays! Because most of the people serach for 🇮🇳 fab during Chinese New Year and Chinese equivalent of dushera season. Most of the fabs in India run overcapacity during this time.

We have grown by more than 3x this year and we remain focused on solving problems that we have,(delay being the major one)

We have also made great progress on our new 10x larger new plant and hope we handle the next season better.

Thanks for your support and patience. Always grateful 🙏

items.description_html:
RT Sarvam
Yesterday, we released Sarvam 30B and Sarvam 105B. Built from scratch, both models leverage a Mixture of Experts (MoE) architecture, delivering stronger performance at scale while using compute more efficiently.

items.title: RT Hemant Mohapatra: Ok, so for anyone who's upset about the @SarvamAI models not beating GPT5/other 1T parameter models, my response: are you f kiddi...
items.description_html:
RT Hemant Mohapatra
Ok, so for anyone who's upset about the @SarvamAI models not beating GPT5/other 1T parameter models, my response: are you f kidding me? A year ago you were crying about India not having our own LLMs trained from scratch. The team has launched 14 amazing products in 14 days and it took years of work, each. And they span text, OCR, voice, LLMs, STT, hardware! I mean hardware! Edge models! Celebrate. They are just getting started. Sure, I am biased as their first investor, but we invested because we thought so highly of them. Of course we are biased. And the odds were enormous. But the opportunity is enormous-er :-) and these guys are absolute killers, and the team hunts! I haven't chatted with @pratykumar today but I heard someone say he saw that PPT for today's LLM launch session literally ON THE STAGE for the first time and laid it all out without a prompter. You can only do it when you know your material cold, and your heart is 100% living and breathing this stuff. Just incredible, congrats to everyone at @SarvamAI. Start of an era.

items.description_html:

My wife wasn’t keen on online schooling or homeschooling Laksh.

I didn’t argue.
Laksh and I kept building. Every day. For four years.

Today she says,
“Regular school isn’t helping him. Let’s give him a better platform.”

Consistency wins. 😇

items.title: India's urban real estate problems are slowly being moved to rural as well, by the same reasons mentioned in this post. In 2021, one acre in Dabbaspet...
items.description_html:
India's urban real estate problems are slowly being moved to rural as well, by the same reasons mentioned in this post.

In 2021, one acre in Dabbaspet industrial area was 1.5cr /KIADB. This was alloted at 37.5L (75% discount) for specific section of people without any limit on it.

Today, one acre in the same KIABD area is 15cr!. Less than 5 years and there are still soo many vacant lands, not much industries running. But the prices are so high, that it's almost impossible for factories to be setup there.

Such artificial price hike up, mostly driven by government is not good for industrial growth!

We need to learn from China.


Sridhar Vembu: How can China be the factory for the world, reach world class in so many industries and still have serious economic issues like the post below describes? China also faces substantial youth unemployment, especially among the college educated, in spite of a shrinking youth

items.title: India: NMDC cuts iron ore prices by up to INR 500/t ($5) for Jul'26 India's largest merchant iron ore mining company, NMDC, has reduced its list price...
items.description_html:
India: NMDC cuts iron ore prices by up to INR 500/t ($5) for Jul'26

India's largest merchant iron ore mining company, NMDC, has reduced its list prices of iron ore CLO (calibrated lump ore) and fines on 10 July 2026, BigMint learnt from sources. The miner has fixed prices of DR CLO (10-40 mm, Fe 67%) at INR 5,850/tonne (t) ($61/t) and of iron ore fines (-10 mm, Fe 64%) at INR 4,700/t ($49/t). Prices are on FOR basis from the miner's Bacheli complex and exclude royalty, DMF, and NMEDT. Prices of all grades have decreased in the range of INR 150-500/t.

items.title: P/B ratio has historically been a more reliable and stable valuation metric for predicting future returns compared to alternatives such as P/E or EV/E...
items.description_html:
P/B ratio has historically been a more reliable and stable valuation metric for predicting future returns compared to alternatives such as P/E or EV/EBITDA. I had demonstrated this through correlation analysis in an earlier investor memo.

The chart below tracks the P/B ratio (blue line) of the Nifty Smallcap 100 Index against the 25th percentile of its trailing 5-year P/B range (orange line). In simple terms, when the blue line falls below the orange line, the current valuation is in the lowest quartile of the past five years.

Since P/B is broadly proportional to ROE, the structural improvement in index ROE—from ~8–12% in the previous decade to ~15% currently—has led to a gradual upward shift in the orange line over time.

Notably, for the first time since the COVID crash, the current P/B has moved below this 25th percentile threshold. Historically, similar instances—during the 2011–2013 downturn, Q1 2016 correction, and the COVID crash—have been followed by strong returns over the subsequent 1–3 years.

This does not imply that markets have bottomed, nor does it guarantee short-term gains. In an event-driven environment, especially with geopolitical risks such as an escalation in war, markets could decline further by 10–20%.

However, what can be said with a reasonably high degree of confidence is that forward-looking returns over a 1–3 year horizon from such valuation levels have historically been better than average.

items.title: In my experience, this is probably the first time the Mid & Small cap indices have outperformed the Nifty 50 during a crisis time. It's also extremely...
items.description_html: In my experience, this is probably the first time the Mid & Small cap indices have outperformed the Nifty 50 during a crisis time. It's also extremely rare to see the Mid & Small cap indices (avg of the two) beating the Nifty 50 near the bottom of the cycle. Their avg CAGR has outperformed during the last two tough years as well as all the below trailing years. I am not that surprised as their balance sheet, P&L and cash flow quality has substantially improved post COVID. I believe the biggest multi-baggers will emerge from this space as the earnings growth differential will be significant going forward. Because of the quality differential today vs during pre-COVID period, I would be surprised if their valuations correct to levels seen during the previous crashes. We probably have the best corporate quality and least stressed financials currently compared to any other previous big crisis.

items.title: Earnings growth picture for Dec'25 quarter vs Dec'24 quarter. Wtd Avg (growth in sum total earnings) is significantly better for the mid and small cap...
items.description_html: Earnings growth picture for Dec'25 quarter vs Dec'24 quarter. Wtd Avg (growth in sum total earnings) is significantly better for the mid and small cap universe. Small cap has an exceptional earnings from Kiri Ind with 5000 cr plus (vs 177 cr year ago) which skews the growth much higher than normal. If you remove that, the wtd avg growth is 14.9%. Median growth reduces influence of such outliers or exceptional items and hence gives a better directional picture. Over all growth is good but nothing exceptional. I believe we should see much improved growth in the Mar'26 quarter as sharp pick up in economic activity (as covered in my last memo) started in the month of December 2025.

items.title: As seen in the table from my latest memo, drawdowns in small caps during the January–March period—measured from the January peak to the February/Mar...
items.description_html:
As seen in the table from my latest memo, drawdowns in small caps during the January–March period—measured from the January peak to the February/March trough—are not an exception; they are a recurring feature of market cycles. Just as consistently, these phases have been followed by sharp recoveries once the market establishes a bottom in February or March.
Current sentiment is such that investors are overly focused on the negatives, making the environment appear so foggy that positives have become invisible. This fog, as always, will eventually lift.

While market bottoms and the depth of drawdowns can never be predicted, it is highly probable that market levels a few months from now will be disproportionately higher. Unfortunately, short-term declines are unsettling for investors, causing many to step aside and miss the eventual strong upswing.

In hindsight, this phase will look like an obvious opportunity. But while living through it, investors see only risks and landmines—never the runway for the next flight

items.title: Markets move through extremes on both sides. These extremes are largely driven by sentiment and, at times, reinforced by fundamentals. Currently, Indi...
items.description_html:
Markets move through extremes on both sides. These extremes are largely driven by sentiment and, at times, reinforced by fundamentals. Currently, Indian equities are experiencing extremely weak sentiment, driven primarily by disappointment over the US trade deal and subdued earnings growth over the past few quarters. However, the September 2025 quarter has shown encouraging signs of earnings growth.

The on-ground feedback I am receiving across various domestic businesses is very strong. For many companies, activity has picked up significantly this month. In my career, I have rarely seen such a wide divergence between market sentiment and on-ground business momentum. After a long time, deep value is emerging in several pockets, even though valuations at the overall market level are not cheap. A shift in sentiment will require a trigger. If not a trade deal, strong earnings and supportive management commentary could serve as a meaningful catalyst.

Wishing everyone a very Happy New Year and hoping for a far more rewarding 2026!

While B started late, he has garnered a good amount

A started early, and he, too, has garnered a good amount

A should top up his SIPs every year or step up his SIPs every year. Person B, too, should do the same

items.title: Samsung Electronics could post its highest-ever profit this year. Riding the ongoing AI boom, the company could generate more operating profit this ye...
items.description_html:
Samsung Electronics could post its highest-ever profit this year.

Riding the ongoing AI boom, the company could generate more operating profit this year than its cumulative operating profit over the past 40 years!

Operating profit for the full year could reach around KRW 300 trillion ($217.4 billion). If Samsung achieves those figures, it could surpass Nvidia’s Q1 2026 operating profit of $59.2 billion, making it one of the world’s most profitable companies

Samsung Electronics acquired Korea Semiconductor in 1974 and began manufacturing integrated circuits (ICs) for LED wristwatches. It later introduced its first 64Kb DRAM chip in 1983 and began selling them in 1984. The company’s cumulative operating profit from 1985 to 2025 was less than KRW 300 trillion (~$217.4 billion).

Samsung plans to invest more than KRW 40 trillion (~$29 billion) annually to meet growing demand for AI semiconductors.

AI/Semiconductor Boom continues!

items.title: This tweet has been coming on my timeline few times now. It's unfortunate, but have three of my learnings on this to share. : 1. Concentrated investin...
items.description_html:
This tweet has been coming on my timeline few times now. It's unfortunate, but have three of my learnings on this to share. :

1. Concentrated investing is a double edged sword - It is not for everyone and doesn't work in 9 out of 10 cases. Most of us would have read stories of wealth creation from concentration, but the truth is that a large part of it is survivorship bias. When you have a super concentrated portfolio, you must be prepared for deep cuts, and frameworks for risk control.

2. Mere cheapness is not a reason to buy - If something is trading "cheap" or below past median valuations, often there is a reason. Growth is the cornerstone of good investing - valuation is secondary. Most investors need better framework of valuations, linked to future and not the past (P/E). Assuming that the market is wrong and you are right is market arrogance.

"It’s pretty hard in a declining business to buy things cheap enough to compensate for the decline."
- Warren Buffett

3. Capacity to suffer - Losses are notional till they are realized. Avoid permanent capital erosion at all costs. Avoid F&O, avoid turnaround bets, avoid investing without manage comfort. All other losses can be recovered. Hence capacity to suffer becomes paramount for investors and also businesses.

Those were my two cents. Happy investing to all.

items.title: IT is up. Everyone is looking for a news or reason. Maybe it's just the realization that pessmism is running ahead of reality (tweeted this yesterday ...
items.description_html:
IT is up. Everyone is looking for a news or reason. Maybe it's just the realization that pessmism is running ahead of reality (tweeted this yesterday also).

Allied reading:
https://www.ndtvprofit.com/technology/palantir-ceo-karp-slams-anthropic-openai-token-pricing-something-has-gone-completely-wrong-11715871
Strong remarks made by Palantir CEO Alex Karp yesterday. criticized the token-based pricing approach used by AI labs like Anthropic and OpenAI, arguing that “something has gone completely wrong.” He said rising AI costs and newer, pricier model generations are frustrating enterprise customers, leading them to avoid “tokenmaxxing” (spending primarily based on tokens) and instead focus on clearer return on investment.

Karp suggested enterprises may shift toward open-weight models that can deliver similar capabilities at lower cost, and he highlighted concerns that China is advancing rapidly in AI development. He also emphasized a trend away from broad, general-purpose AI and toward more efficient, proprietary tools tailored to specific businesses.

In line with Palantir’s broader stance, Karp positioned open-weight approaches (including Palantir’s work with Nvidia) as a solution—framing the key need for buyers as control over compute, models, and data (the “means of production”), rather than handing that control to external providers.

items.title: What Chamath is saying below is not wrong, but it is nothing new - maybe 2 years late, and doesn't add to the discourse. Yes, the old model of T&M (bi...
items.description_html:
What Chamath is saying below is not wrong, but it is nothing new - maybe 2 years late, and doesn't add to the discourse. Yes, the old model of T&M (billing by the hour) is ending and shifting towards outcomes based pricing. The cost of new code generation is down by 80%, but it comes at its own cost (resources and community cost).

If the Enterprise world were to be built completely from scratch today, the "AI disrupts everything" argument would be true. As things stand today, there is a massive delta between how exponentially fast AI models are improving and how fast legacy multi-billion-dollar enterprises can meaningfully absorb them. This is creating a massive Enterprise deployment gap (refer image below from Infosys AI Investor day). This gap is a $300-400 Billion opportunity for Technology Services companies - albeit not IT players would benefit from this. There should be good value for IT players who can deliver the conversion layer with an AI-ready approach.

So far, the ROI for companies using AI instead of humans is not there and this may change in another year if the AI capex does not slow down. But as investors, at some point, we must ask - is the pessimism on IT running ahead of Enterprise reality?


Chamath Palihapitiya: Show me the incentive and I’ll show you the outcome.

Destroyed assets include aircraft hangars, fuel depots, munitions storage, and critical satellite communication (SATCOM) facilities. items.title: RT SOIC Research: Today we are going to discuss a very unique 2W OEM that has been able to built a product that is built to last in 10+ years. For con...
items.description_html:
RT SOIC Research
Today we are going to discuss a very unique 2W OEM that has been able to built a product that is built to last in 10+ years. For context - They had no legacy nothing and have built the business ground up from nothing (product first distribution later)

Ather invested in the things that compound and are hard to copy i.e. design IP, software, brand, charging network and rented the things that don't (cell manufacturing). That is why it spends less than Ola yet has better unit economics, and why the moat is earned rather than bought.

And this is the fruits of investing in building technology and a superior product for years before just thinking about scaling distribution. At the end of the day we can see the result that the product won that did not catch fire :)

But here today we will get into details of how they created this MOAT and how it is now able to win a superior market share and show such a strong growth at the same time when competition did not decrease in fact it has gone up!

items.title: RT Synthetic Sciences (YC W26): Introducing OpenScience. A better, open-source Claude Science. • Any model: GLM, Kimi, DeepSeek, Claude, GPT, your ow...
items.description_html:
RT Synthetic Sciences (YC W26)
Introducing OpenScience. A better, open-source Claude Science.

• Any model: GLM, Kimi, DeepSeek, Claude, GPT, your own fine-tune. Switching is one flag.
• 250+ research skills across ML, comp bio, cheminformatics. All readable, editable, extensible.
• No throttling, no gatekeeping, no one vendor deciding what science is okay.
• Native Atlas integration: many agents, one shared reproducible research graph.
• Runs on your infra. Your data stays yours.

Scientific AI should be open. One company shouldn't own the tools the rest of us discover with, or decide who gets to.

items.description_html: Telangana has India's highest #Csection rate (62.2%) and a maternal mortality ratio of 48 deaths per 100,000 live births. Tamil Nadu (47% C-section rate) and Kerala (41%) report far lower MMRs—25 and 24. More #surgery hasn't meant safer births: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: Karnataka's #Csection rate (46%) is nearly identical to Tamil Nadu's (47%). But its maternal mortality ratio—73 per 100,000 live births—is nearly th...
items.description_html: Karnataka's #Csection rate (46%) is nearly identical to Tamil Nadu's (47%). But its maternal mortality ratio—73 per 100,000 live births—is nearly three times higher. #Surgery rates alone don't explain outcomes, @PrachiSalve11 reports: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: A normal delivery can take 6-12+ hours of monitoring. A C-section is scheduled and done in far less time. Experts point to this "arithmetic," plus the...

In Tamil Nadu, private #childbirth costs nearly Rs 70,000, about 50 times the public-hospital cost. Commercialisation, rising #Csections are to blame: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

Experts say private maternity care is increasingly the default even outside big cities—reshaping how women experience childbirth: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: Jammu & Kashmir has the highest private-hospital C-section rate in India at 90%, followed by West Bengal (88%) and Telangana (84%). Nationally, privat...
items.description_html: Jammu & Kashmir has the highest private-hospital C-section rate in India at 90%, followed by West Bengal (88%) and Telangana (84%). Nationally, private hospitals deliver more than half of all births by #surgery: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: The @WHO says C-section rates above 10% don't improve maternal or #newborn survival at a population level. India's #private hospitals are at 54%. Publ...
items.description_html: The @WHO says C-section rates above 10% don't improve maternal or #newborn survival at a population level. India's #private hospitals are at 54%. Public hospitals, at 17%, are close to the "optimal" range. @PrachiSalve11 writes: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: Happy to see my work in @thewire_in GST’s key feature: it’s self-enforcing. Firms need invoices to claim credits → they demand co...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
Happy to see my work in @thewire_in

GST’s key feature: it’s self-enforcing. Firms need invoices to claim credits → they demand compliance from suppliers → formalisation cascades through supply chains.

https://thewire.in/economy/how-the-gst-created-a-formalisation-cascade

from @Ideas4India

Paper
https://meghapatnaik.github.io/research/gst_prowess.pdf

Thrilled to share our paper on Sustainable Remote Workplaces (with @BeataJechorek , @asdurso & @ChloeThurstonDC ) is forthcoming in JSIS.

We show employees evaluate remote work as bundles of trade-offs: flexibility, pay, monitoring, & costs are considered jointly

#FutureOfWork

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: new blog post at @Ideas4India 45% of India’s manufacturing is informal. My work shows GST is slowly changing this: an input credit ...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
new blog post at @Ideas4India 45% of India’s manufacturing is informal. My work shows GST is slowly changing this: an input credit system creates a formalisation cascade where firms demand invoices from suppliers & this pulls the entire supply chain slowly into the formal economy


Ideas for India: Value-added tax has a self-enforcing design that generates formalisation cascades through supply chains. Using @_CMIE Prowess data on 12,024 firms for the period 2013-2022, Megha Patnaik @megonomista examines impact of India’s introduction of GST in 2017
https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/macroeconomics/how-the-gst-created-a-formalisation-cascade

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: Global Capability Centres are projected to contribute to 3.5% of India's GDP. My new paper argues that GCCs are firm-level reorganis...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
Global Capability Centres are projected to contribute to 3.5% of India's GDP. My new paper argues that GCCs are firm-level reorganisation at the global scale: only the most productive firms set up GCCs, trading off lower wages against higher coordination costs of remote work.


CEPR: New CEPR Discussion Paper - DP21294
Services Across Borders: How Firms Organize Remote Work at Scale
Megha Patnaik @megonomista @UniLUISS
https://ow.ly/2gIf50YuL4m
#CEPR_ITRE #EconTwitter

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: 45% of India's GDP is informal. My new paper shows GST's credit mechanism is pulling firms into the formal economy, not through enfo...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
45% of India's GDP is informal. My new paper shows GST's credit mechanism is pulling firms into the formal economy, not through enforcement but because every buyer demands an invoice from the supplier behind it.
Formalisation cascading begins with large firms and grows upstream


CEPR: New CEPR Discussion Paper - DP21163
Self-Enforcing Tax Design and Supply Chain Formalization: Evidence from India’s GST Reform
Megha Patnaik @megonomista @UniLUISS
https://ow.ly/5QK550YfcL1
#CEPR_PE #EconTwitter

RT Rahul Shukla

Ideas for India: Despite ⬆️productivity in formal manufacturing in India, wages have been⬇️ or stagnant.

@rahulshuklamzp @The_IGC shows that in a given year, the more productive a state’s formal manufacturing sector, the smaller the share of that output flows to workers

items.title: Equirus says Dixon secures long-awaited Vivo JV approval is a major growth catalyst Explained below 1. Vivo shipments: 22.2mn (CY23) → 25.1mn (CY24) ...
items.description_html:
Equirus says Dixon secures long-awaited Vivo JV approval is a major growth catalyst

Explained below
1. Vivo shipments: 22.2mn (CY23) → 25.1mn (CY24) → 29.3mn (CY25), with market share rising 15% → 17% → 19%

2. Management previously indicated the JV could eventually manufacture ~2/3 of Vivo India's volumes = 18–20mn smartphones annually.

3. Dixon produced ~33mn smartphones in FY26. So at full ramp-up, the Vivo JV alone could lift volumes by ~55–60%, reinforcing its leadership in India's smartphone EMS market.

4. FY27 guidance of ~₹56,000cr revenue & 32–33mn smartphone volumes excluded any Vivo contribution.

5. Equirus expects production to begin in 3QFY27, with 8–10mn incremental units in FY27 & a 18–20mn annualized run-rate as the JV scales.

Bottom line: The Vivo JV is a meaningful incremental growth driver beyond Dixon's existing FY27 guidance.


Nigel D'Souza: 🚨 Dixon Technologies and Vivo have signed JV agreements to expand smartphone manufacturing in India 🇮🇳

Details below
- Dixon to own 51%; Vivo Mobile India to hold 49%
- GoI has approved Vivo's investment, clearing the way for incorporation
- JV will manufacture Vivo smartphones

items.description_html:
India rebounds in Jun'26: Can FII flows sustain? 🇮🇳

FII outflows moderated to USD5.2bn in Jun, with a sharp turnaround to USD1.3bn net buying in 2H June

Nearly two-thirds of outflows came from O&G, Autos and Metals, while Financials, Services and Consumer Durables attracted inflows.

What is strengthening India's investment case?
Easing geopolitical risks, softer crude, improving earnings and relatively attractive valuations. As AI-led capital broadens beyond the initial capex cycle, India could see a more sustained recovery in foreign flows.

Source @MotilalOswalLtd

items.title: CareEdge Ratings was proud to be the Knowledge Partner at the @ASSOCHAM4India 17th Mutual Fund Summit, held on July 3, 2026, in Delhi, where industry ...
items.description_html:
CareEdge Ratings was proud to be the Knowledge Partner at the @ASSOCHAM4India 17th Mutual Fund Summit, held on July 3, 2026, in Delhi, where industry leaders came together to discuss the evolving landscape of India's asset management ecosystem.

The summit was graced by the Chief Guest, Shri Amarjeet Singh, Whole Time Director, Securities and Exchange Board of India (@SEBI_India).

A key highlight of the summit was the release of our knowledge report, “Mutual Funds – Capitalising on India's Ascent for Viksit Bharat”, offering insights into the sector's growth trajectory and its role in supporting India's long-term economic aspirations.

Our experts played an active role in driving meaningful conversations:

Sanjay Agarwal, Senior Director at CareEdge Ratings, delivered a Special Address and moderated an engaging session on "Investment and Distribution Strategies: The Road Ahead in a Dynamic Market," exploring the evolving landscape of investor engagement and distribution.

Saurabh Bhalerao, Director, CareEdge Ratings, participated as a panellist in "Leveraging Technology & FinTech Disruption: Innovation, Opportunities & Challenges, and Investor Education," sharing perspectives on how technology is reshaping the mutual fund ecosystem.

#MutualFunds #AssetManagement #InvestmentStrategy #FinTech #InvestorEducation #CapitalMarkets #ViksitBharat #IndiaGrowthStory #ThoughtLeadership #CareEdgeRatings #BFSI #EconomicGrowth

𝗜𝘀 𝗜𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗮'𝘀 𝗙𝗗𝗜 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹𝗹𝘆 𝗱𝗲𝗰𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴? 𝗢𝗿 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝘄𝗲 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗻𝘂𝗺𝗯𝗲𝗿𝘀 𝘄𝗿𝗼𝗻𝗴?

𝗪𝗵𝗲𝗻 𝗻𝗲𝘁 𝗙𝗗𝗜 𝗳𝗹𝗼𝘄𝘀 𝗮𝗽𝗽𝗲𝗮𝗿 𝘁𝗼 𝗯𝗲 𝗳𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴, 𝗱𝗼𝗲𝘀 𝗶𝘁 𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝘄𝗲𝗮𝗸𝗲𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻𝘃𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗳𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲, 𝗼𝗿 𝗶𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗿𝗲 𝗮 𝗯𝗶𝗴𝗴𝗲𝗿 𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗯𝗲𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗮?

CareEdge’s latest sector update points to a clear shift in industry dynamics, where growth is becoming more concentrated, and more competitive.

Key takeaways:

-Retail-led momentum strengthening: Industry premiums grew 8.7% y-o-y in May 2026, driven by health (↑13.7%) and motor segments, with retail health alone expanding 30.5% y-o-y.

-Private insurers & SAHIs gaining share: Private players grew 11.8%, while SAHIs surged 31.7%, pushing their combined market share to 70.9%—highlighting a decisive tilt toward customer-centric, retail-focused models.

-Segment divergence persists: Sharp contractions in fire (-24.5%) and crop insurance continue to offset broader gains, underscoring volatility in commercial and government-linked lines.

Read Report: https://www.careratings.com/uploads/newsfiles/1782196581_Health%20and%20Private%20Insurers%20Lead%20Non-Life%20Premium%20Growth%20in%20May%202026.pdf

#CareEdge #InsuranceSector #NonLifeInsurance #HealthInsurance #MotorInsurance #BFSI #SectorOutlook #RiskManagement #FinancialServices #IndiaEconomy

items.title: In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY. Exports to China ...

Exports to China fell for the 11th consecutive month, with China’s share of U.S. exports dropping to 3.8%. Exports to Taiwan and India rose YoY.

items.title: Corporate insiders are buying tech stocks at a record pace: 28 executives at companies within the US technology sector ETF, $XLK, have purchased their...
items.description_html:
Corporate insiders are buying tech stocks at a record pace:

28 executives at companies within the US technology sector ETF, $XLK, have purchased their own stock on the open market over the last 6 months, the highest count on record, according to SentimenTrader.

This figure has DOUBLED since the start of 2026.

This also surpasses the previous record of 25 insiders set in 2011.

By comparison, in early 2025, just 5 executives were buyers.

US executives are rushing to buy tech stocks.

items.title: BREAKING: Total US oil product exports surged to a record 8.7 million barrels per day last week. The increase was led by propane, followed by diesel, ...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Total US oil product exports surged to a record 8.7 million barrels per day last week.

The increase was led by propane, followed by diesel, gasoline, and jet fuel cargoes.

Most US diesel exports are headed to Brazil and the rest of South America, while ~14% is bound for Europe.

Since March, US oil product exports have risen +2.0 million barrels per day, or +30%.

By comparison, in early 2022, refined product shipments briefly fell below 4.0 million barrels per day.

This comes despite weekly crude oil exports declining -3.1 million barrels per day from their April peak, to 3.3 million barrels per day, close to the average levels seen over the last 2 years.

Global demand for American fuel is skyrocketing.

items.title: BREAKING: US consumer credit fell -$182 million in May, the first monthly decline since November 2024. This was significantly below expectations of a ...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: US consumer credit fell -$182 million in May, the first monthly decline since November 2024.

This was significantly below expectations of a +$17.5 billion increase and followed a +$20.8 billion increase in April.

The decline was driven by revolving credit, which includes credit cards, plunging -$5.3 billion, the 2nd-largest monthly drop since November 2020.

This comes after a +$11.5 billion and +$10.7 billion increase in April and March.

At the same time, non-revolving credit, which includes auto and student loans, jumped +$5.1 billion, the smallest monthly increase since February.

Meanwhile, the average interest rate on credit cards rose to 22.15%, near the highest on record.

Is consumer borrowing reaching a tipping point?

items.title: The AI arms race is driving record Big Tech borrowing: Amazon $AMZN, Alphabet $GOOGL, Nvidia $NVDA, Meta $META, Oracle $ORCL, and SpaceX, $SPCX, have ...
items.description_html:
The AI arms race is driving record Big Tech borrowing:

Amazon $AMZN, Alphabet $GOOGL, Nvidia $NVDA, Meta $META, Oracle $ORCL, and SpaceX, $SPCX, have issued a record $182 billion in investment-grade bonds so far in 2026.

This marks a +1,300% increase from ~$13 billion over the same period in 2025.

As a result, these 6 firms account for nearly 15% of total US corporate bond issuance year-to-date and over 50% of this year's growth in corporate bond issuance.

Meanwhile, a record 7 bond deals of $25 billion or more have taken place during this period, matching the total number of deals seen between 2019 and 2025.

6 of the 7 deals came from these 6 companies, with the remaining one coming from Salesforce, $CRM.

AI capital needs are reshaping the corporate bond market.

items.title: US semiconductor funds are seeing unprecedented demand: The semiconductor ETF, $SOXX, attracted +$5.4 billion in inflows on Tuesday, the largest daily...
items.description_html:
US semiconductor funds are seeing unprecedented demand:

The semiconductor ETF, $SOXX, attracted +$5.4 billion in inflows on Tuesday, the largest daily intake since the fund’s launch in 2001.

This is more than +300% above the previous record daily inflow.

The 3x leveraged long semiconductor ETF, $SOXL, posted +$1.2 billion in inflows, the 2nd-largest daily inflow this year.

In total, US long semiconductor ETFs took in +$7.1 billion in fresh capital on Tuesday.

Year-to-date, $SOXX alone has posted +$13.3 billion in inflows.

Investors are buying the dip in semiconductors at an unprecedented pace.

items.title: BREAKING: The top 10 US stocks now account for 43% of the S&P 500’s market cap, near the highest on record. This percentage has been at or above 40% ...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The top 10 US stocks now account for 43% of the S&P 500’s market cap, near the highest on record.

This percentage has been at or above 40% for the last 12 months.

Over the last 10 years, the top 10's weighting in the S&P 500 has more than DOUBLED.

At the same time, the proportion of the 250 smallest companies has halved, to ~7%, the lowest since at least 2014.

To put this differently, the top 10 now account for more than 6 times the market cap of the smallest 250 firms in the S&P 500 index.

A handful of stocks continue to effectively drive the entire market.

items.title: BREAKING: The Index of US Financial Conditions is up to ~1.2 points, the easiest since February 2026. This is also near the highest level in at least ...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The Index of US Financial Conditions is up to ~1.2 points, the easiest since February 2026.

This is also near the highest level in at least 11 years.

Since 2015, similar readings have only been recorded in early 2025 and during 2021, before the Fed started hiking rates.

The Financial Conditions Index has risen over +1.0 points since the March low.

Most of this surge has come from rising equity markets and falling corporate bond spreads.

Financial conditions are easing despite the recent increase in inflation.

items.title: Tech stocks are experiencing one of their most volatile periods in history: The ratio of the Nasdaq 100 volatility index, $VXN, to the S&P 500 volatil...
items.description_html:
Tech stocks are experiencing one of their most volatile periods in history:

The ratio of the Nasdaq 100 volatility index, $VXN, to the S&P 500 volatility index, $VIX, is up to 1.7 points, the highest in 23 years.

This marks the first time since 2018 that the ratio has surpassed 1.5 points.

By comparison, during the 2008 Financial Crisis, this metric peaked at ~1.6 points.

This comes as $VXN stands at 28 points while the $VIX is at 16 points, or 43% lower.

$VXN has now been above 20 points for 5 consecutive months, the longest streak since the 2022 bear market.

The market is pricing-in severe volatility in tech.

items.title: Agentic AI continues to see rapid growth. Over the last 12 months, the focus has rapidly shifted toward the software layer that allows AI to connect w...
items.description_html:
Agentic AI continues to see rapid growth.

Over the last 12 months, the focus has rapidly shifted toward the software layer that allows AI to connect with enterprise data and applications.

Instead of simply answering questions, these systems can access company databases, analyze documents, execute multi-step tasks, and automate entire business processes with minimal human input.

In other words, AI is evolving from a chatbot into a digital employee.

As a result, the enterprise AI market is now expected to exceed $155 billion by 2030.

This is why virtually every major technology company is racing to build AI agents.

The next phase of the AI Revolution has arrived.

items.title: American consumers are becoming increasingly bullish on equities: The perceived probability among US consumers that US stock prices will be higher 1-y...
items.description_html:
American consumers are becoming increasingly bullish on equities:

The perceived probability among US consumers that US stock prices will be higher 1-year from now increased +2.9 percentage points in June, to 40.9%, the highest since April 2021.

This is also the 3rd-highest reading since the 2020 pandemic recovery.

By age, consumers under 40 are the most optimistic, at 43.1%, the highest since May 2021.

This is followed by the 40-59 and over 59 age groups, both at 40.2%, the highest readings this year.

By income, the perceived probability of higher stock prices rose to 48.3% among those earning over $100,000, the highest since January 2025.

This compares with 38.6% among those earning $50,000-$100,000 and 33.6% among those earning under $50,000, both in-line with the average over the last 2 years.

High-income American consumers are historically bullish.

items.title: Gold is entering a historically favorable period of the year: Gold prices have gained +1.5% in July on average over the last 20 years, making it the 2...
items.description_html:
Gold is entering a historically favorable period of the year:

Gold prices have gained +1.5% in July on average over the last 20 years, making it the 2nd-strongest month of the year.

Gold prices have also recorded positive returns in 65% of July months, the 2nd-best win rate of any month.

The strongest July over this period was in 2020, when gold returned +10.7%.

The only better month historically has been August, with an average gain of +1.6% since 2005.

August also posted a 55% win rate, with its strongest year coming in 2011, when gold returned +12.1%.

By comparison, June has historically been the weakest month, with an average decline of -0.4% and just 40% positive readings since 2005.

Seasonality is turning in favor of gold bulls.

items.title: BREAKING: Tokenized equities saw a record $3.4 billion in volume for the month of June. This marks +279% month-over-month growth and +1,400% year-over...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Tokenized equities saw a record $3.4 billion in volume for the month of June.

This marks +279% month-over-month growth and +1,400% year-over-year growth.

The growth was primarily driven by SpaceX's record IPO and surging demand for 24/7 trading.

Furthermore, the Solana network now accounts for over 90% of volume traded in these assets.

A substantial amount of this volume growth has been driven by Jupiter, the largest onchain platform in the world, which has seen MoM volume growth of +56%, with ~60% of tokenized equity volume happening during off hours and weekends.

Tokenized asset growth is exploding.

items.title: Iran/US - Is it a blip or a breakdown ? @PramitWorld says that Iran felt betrayed because, despite the ceasefire, potential buyers were not buying the...
items.description_html:
Iran/US - Is it a blip or a breakdown ?

@PramitWorld says that Iran felt betrayed because, despite the ceasefire, potential buyers were not buying their oil due to a lack of "definite clearance" from US Treasury.

10 points from our chat today. Video below.

1. MoU was very vague to start with, a kind of a 'throw-together peace'

2. A clash or flare-up was inevitable at some point

3. Imposing sanctions is easy, undoing them is very difficult

4. Iranian oil were passing via SoH but with no buyers of the oil, due to US sanctions, Iran would have felt betrayed

5. Potential buyers of Iranian oil would have needed extraordinary level of assurance that they are not violating sanctions

6. Iran has pushed the envelope quite a bit w.r.t. SoH, emboldened by Guld states

7. I think the core position of U.S. remains the same - it isn't ready to use military force to reopen the SoH

8. I would imagine Iranian influence in the region would keep growing

9. Trump wants to get out of the war, keep US pump prices low, with a difficult midterms ahead - these reasons have not changed

10. Markets may take the long view that there is enough oil in the world, the Saudis want to pump a lot more & that no one wants to really fight.

#Nifty #BankNifty #Trump #IranWar‌ #Oil

items.title: I finally have an announcement to make! I've been creating content for almost a decade, and so many of you have told me my work has impacted your inve...
items.description_html:
I finally have an announcement to make!

I've been creating content for almost a decade, and so many of you have told me my work has impacted your investing in some way. That means everything to me!

So a little over a year ago I decided to formalize things and got SEBI-registered. For a finance content creator already doing well, you might ask why add all that compliance and complexity. Simple, I felt quality stock market research that is also accountable was missing.

A lot of you in my circle have heard me mention an app I've been building. Since then I've built a team of around 20, mostly in research (fundamental and technical), plus tech, customer success, and operations.

Together, we built @RecoWealth, a platform and community for Quality Equity Research, Curated Stock Recommendations with Transparency, and a genuine community around all things investing in India and US.

We think most people are underexposed to equity in general, and even more so to global markets.

We didn't want to be just another place for stock tips so we've vertically integrated to be with you from the start of your investing journey and as a partner throughout.

Time to change the way people look at Research! We are coming soon with our App! Stay Tuned.....

items.description_html:
June auto sales data is out: Autos are performing strong as expected.

Tata PV was the biggest winner, with EV sales almost 3x higher than last year, helping it post the strongest growth.

Hyundai's sales fell mainly due to a fire at one of its plants, which affected production.

Mahindra continues to dominate the SUV segment, while Maruti Suzuki remains the market leader with its value-focused portfolio.

items.title: One thing we always track is the promoter's stake! When promoters keep increasing their stake, it often signals confidence in the business. Paisalo Di...
items.description_html:
One thing we always track is the promoter's stake!

When promoters keep increasing their stake, it often signals confidence in the business.

Paisalo Digital is a good example. Promoter holding has steadily increased from ~26% in FY19 to 46.72% in Q1FY27.

Also, the company has guided for doubling its AUM, income, and PAT over the next 3 years, and the stock is up 20% today.

Disclosure: We have Paisalo Digital in our FLUID Q Smallcase basket.

items.description_html:
RT Timsy Jaipuria
Nine years after GST reshaped India's indirect tax landscape, gross GST collections for June 2026 came in at ₹1.95 lakh crore, up 13.9% YoY.

This 13.9% y-o-y increase, as per data, primarily looks to be driven by a sharp jump in import revenues.

Imports GST show a growth of about 34%, which experts say, could be, to some extent, on account of rupee depreciation.

Overall growth however depicts strong resilience by the Indian economy amidst geopolitical uncertainties

Govt data 👇

items.title: TCS numbers, my thoughts on IT sentiment 1) AI revenues are growing , BFSI has seen good recovery this quarter 2) SKF mega deal net new deal for trans...
items.description_html:
TCS numbers, my thoughts on IT sentiment

1) AI revenues are growing , BFSI has seen good recovery this quarter
2) SKF mega deal net new deal for transformation services with AI first mindset
3) Seeing some good deal wins on the agentic front as well on the HR /Finance side
4) Expecting some growth to also come back in Q2 with recovery in some accounts across life sciences and other verticals

Problems remain on overall growth being lower is still there , but the degrowth or getting worse scenario odds are reducing seem to be for now

IT is no more a local theme because after this recent correction , accenture is also now cheaper than TCS on valuations so options are many , very little ownership interest also for now

For now you all know we have been preffering on the Midcap side with players like Rategain , Indegne and Coforge as my picks and are doing well also especially rategain

But I am watching the sentiment also on Largecap , spoke to few FII's also , everyone seems to be in wait and watch mode here

One line : it is not as bad as it may seem , but right question is if it is good enough to own?

I mostly read these to get a view on NIFTY EPS estimates to find where the next driver comes from and who can lead the rally

items.title: Every few quarters , I look back at companies that had given 25-30% CAGR growth guidance , which these days companies love to give to analysts , becau...
items.description_html:
Every few quarters , I look back at companies that had given 25-30% CAGR growth guidance , which these days companies love to give to analysts , because its so easy to come up with imaginary numbers

I personally think a lot of it is a vicious loop ( companies that guide aggressively also attract the type of investors who play these lottery games, and it works for the company and the analyst , can raise funding at higher multiples , can come up with some fictional reports to upgrade the stock ) because the fragility in the world is so high that any small event can disrupt global supply chains and break down business models in a few weeks , the fact being , even businesses after a certain extent do not know the future

So I look at some analysts who take 30% guidance and then model it for 5 years and then come up with some imaginary number and then say is cheap/expensive , which is a classic example of illusion of certainty

When the market is so dynamic , don't fall for the illusion of certainty, it will in most cases disappoint just as a sample if you took 30 companies that had guided for 35-30% CAGR growth , maybe 20 do it in the first year , 15 in the next , but over a 3 year period making forecasts on the basis of guidance is a pretty useless exercise , and its not the companies fault , that's the nature of the game

Now , in my opinion the better approach is : Guidance is not a bad thing to give , I also do track it but there is weightage and it always and always has to be looked at in context , Gauge the probability of execution continuing and understand the market dynamic and assess probability and create a execution -probability matrix , it will be far easier and more rewarding as well

Think in scenarios to valuation today , and the odds of how long an execution cycle lasts in that industry and then does the valuation come in the context of incremental size of profit pool to reverse engineer the growth , in most cases you can tell in less than a minute that this is a high probability case or a pathetic hope based investment Just my opinion

items.title: Auto Ancillaries: Premiumisation Driving Higher Content Per Vehicle Rising consumer demand for premium features is increasing the value of components ...
items.description_html:
Auto Ancillaries: Premiumisation Driving Higher Content Per Vehicle

Rising consumer demand for premium features is increasing the value of components per vehicle, creating a structural growth driver for auto ancillary companies.
Connected features are expected to see the fastest adoption, rising from 2% (FY19) to 50% (FY27E).
Sunroof penetration is projected to increase from 10% to 40% over FY19–FY27E.
Automatic transmission (AT) adoption is expected to grow from 17% to 35%, while alloy wheels rise to 50% penetration.
LED lighting penetration is also expected to increase from 20% to 35% by FY27E.
Higher feature content translates into higher vehicle ASPs and greater revenue opportunity for component manufacturers, even if overall vehicle volumes grow at a moderate pace.

items.title: Indo Borax: ₹20 Cr Boron Oxide Capex to Move Up the Value Chain Indo Borax is building its next phase of growth around 4 clear priorities — Boric Ac...
items.description_html:
Indo Borax: ₹20 Cr Boron Oxide Capex to Move Up the Value Chain

Indo Borax is building its next phase of growth around 4 clear priorities — Boric Acid leadership, Boron Oxide expansion, exports and R&D.

- Company will continue to strengthen its leadership in Boric Acid

- Focus remains on capacity enhancement and operational improvements

- Indo Borax is investing ~₹20 crore in a new Boron Oxide facility at Pithampur

- Planned Boron Oxide capacity: 4,000 MTPA

- This will help the company move into higher-margin Boron chemistry products

- International expansion is another key priority

- Focus markets include South America and South-East Asia

- R&D investments will be increased for specialised boron-based solutions

- Target applications include advanced materials, flame retardants and specialty agriculture

Key takeaway:

Indo Borax is trying to evolve from a Boric Acid-led company into a broader Boron chemistry platform.

The ₹20 crore Boron Oxide capex is small in size, but strategically important — it pushes the company towards higher-margin products and newer global markets.


Beat The Street: Indo Borax - Leader in Boric Acid

An import substitution play on new and emerging chemical products - Expanding Products and Integration

Going through a strategic transformation

Relationship managers increased to 417 vs 382 YoY

Active client families increased to 13,941 vs 12,330 YoY

This shows that many HNI clients are gradually moving towards the UHNI segment due to wallet share gains & portfolio growth

At the same time, growth is also moderating slightly from earlier levels

Even if the company surpasses its FY27 guidance, stock still trades at 76x earnings

items.title: Many of these stocks were once popular ideas What is the common pattern in this list? At some point, they had strong narratives, huge potential & were...
items.description_html:
Many of these stocks were once popular ideas

What is the common pattern in this list?

At some point, they had strong narratives, huge potential & were widely discussed

But today, hardly anyone talks about them

Why? because most of them have witnessed massive drawdowns (60-80%) from their all time highs

This is where the real learning lies

If you want to learn investing, study why these stocks became popular, what the original thesis was, what went wrong & why the idea did not work out

You will find multiple reasons - growth slowdown, valuation de-rating, poor execution, governance issues, weak cash flows, industry headwinds or simply expectations running too far ahead of reality etc

On social media, stocks mostly get discussed when they are doing well

Once they start falling, nobody wants to talk about them (this is the harsh reality)

For every 1 buy & hold success story, there are hundreds of stocks where buy & hold did not work out

That is why tracking is extremely important

Buy >> Track >> Review

These were once very popular ideas, but have now seen sharp drawdowns

The objective is simple - understand what went wrong & what investors can learn from them
